baseline_guidance: Run each eval with this skill and compare against a no-skill baseline or the previous skill version; pass only when the skilled run better satisfies the assertions without losing required safety or precision.

evals.prompt: Review this proposed refactor: extract a UserService with methods matching every current helper function so tests can mock each helper independently. Is that a good design?
evals.expected_output: The agent evaluates the design using deep-module vocabulary, rejects a shallow pass-through interface, and recommends a smaller interface tested through the seam.
assertions: Uses the terms module, interface, depth, seam, adapter, leverage, and locality consistently.
Applies the deletion test to identify pass-through or shallow design.
Explains that the interface is the test surface.
Avoids substituting component, service, API, or boundary as the core vocabulary.

evals.prompt: Should I add an interface around this database repository even though there is only one Postgres implementation and no fake yet?
evals.expected_output: The agent distinguishes a hypothetical seam from a real seam and recommends introducing the seam only when variation or tests make it earn its cost.
assertions: Uses the rule that one adapter means a hypothetical seam and two adapters means a real one.
Separates seam placement from implementation internals.
Discusses testability through a public interface rather than private internals.
Frames the tradeoff in terms of leverage and locality.
